sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics Stellar P6 MCUs Targeted at Next-gen EV Platforms
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has introduced its new Stellar P6 automotive MCUs, designed for next-generation electric vehicles (EVs). These MCUs are the first qualifiable devices for model year 2024 to integrate the CAN-XL in-car communication standard, enabling efficient handling of increasing data flows in EVs. The Stellar P series combines advanced actuation capabilities with powerful function integration, specifically targeting new drivetrain trends and domain-oriented architectures for optimal real-time performance and energy management in electric vehicles.

feed.title: Intel Unveils New Chips and Software as It Chases a Comeback (INTC)
feed.summary: Intel Corp. introduced new personal-computer processors, graphics semiconductors, and software as part of its strategy to regain its position in the chip industry. The new lineup includes Core desktop processors for high-performance users and a graphics chip named Ponte Vecchio for data centers, aimed at challenging Nvidia. These announcements were made at Intel's Innovate event, showcasing their efforts towards a comeback.

feed.title: Micron’s big new Boise fab will slow US economy, bring higher taxes and worse
feed.summary: Economist Peter Crabb argues that Micron Technology's new $15 billion Boise semiconductor plant, despite being seen as an economic boon, will likely slow the U.S. economy and lead to higher taxes due to federal subsidies and government borrowing. He also suggests that Micron's shareholders will face higher costs and a lower return on investment, as indicated by the company's stock performance and low price-to-earnings ratio compared to peers. Crabb emphasizes the often-overlooked long-term economic consequences of such large-scale, government-subsidized projects.

feed.title: Meet the Harvard Law School graduate helping China cut its reliance on US AI chips
feed.summary: Michael Zhang Wen, a Harvard Law School graduate and chairman of Biren Technology, is leading China's efforts to reduce its dependence on US artificial intelligence (AI) chips. His Shanghai-based start-up recently released the BR100, a 7-nanometre GPU, which some believe could position Biren to become "China's Nvidia" amidst escalating US export restrictions on advanced chips. Zhang faces pressure to deliver, as his company has raised a substantial US$655 million from major venture capital funds.

feed.title: SoftBank’s Son expected to meet Samsung's Lee for Arm M&A discussion
feed.summary: SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son is anticipated to meet with Samsung Electronics de facto leader Jay Y. Lee to discuss a potential sale or strategic alliance for British chip designer Arm. This meeting follows a collapsed deal to sell Arm to Nvidia due to regulatory hurdles and a broader industry consensus that a single company acquisition might be difficult. Various scenarios are being considered, including a consortium of chipmakers or a partial acquisition by Samsung, though some industry watchers express skepticism about significant outcomes given Arm's high valuation and SoftBank's financial state.

feed.title: TSMC remains world’s largest contract chipmaker
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) maintained its position as the world's largest contract chipmaker in Q2, holding a 53.4% market share despite a slight dip from the previous quarter. The company recorded $18.15 billion in sales due to demand from high-performance computing, IoT, and automotive electronics. South Korea's Samsung Electronics and Taiwan's United Microelectronics Corp followed with significant market shares.

feed.title: Meta launches AI software tools to ease switching between Nvidia, AMD chips
feed.summary: Meta Platforms Inc. has launched new open-source AI software tools to enable easier switching between different underlying chips from Nvidia and AMD. These tools, based on PyTorch, can significantly speed up code execution on both Nvidia A100 and AMD MI250 chips, offering developers greater flexibility and reducing migration costs between hardware vendors. This initiative aims to address the vendor lock-in common with Nvidia's CUDA platform and improve hardware choice for deep learning developers.

feed.title: Intel’s Journey From 100 Billion To 1 Trillion Transistors Was Painted At The IntelON 2022 Event
feed.summary: During the IntelON 2022 event, CEO Pat Gelsinger outlined Intel's ambitious goal to increase transistor count from 100 billion to 1 trillion by the end of the decade, reinforcing Moore's Law. The company also announced significant advancements, including new data center and gaming GPUs (Flex Series, Ponte Vecchio, Arc A770), and the 13th Gen Core Processors (Raptor Lake). Intel's focus remains on an end-to-end foundry approach, expansion of the UCIe consortium for open chiplet ecosystems, and strengthening its offerings in both consumer and enterprise markets.

feed.title: Austin Ekeler of the Los Angeles Chargers Introduces the EKLR Signature Gaming PC Exclusively from Newegg
feed.summary: Newegg has launched the EKLR PC, a signature gaming computer endorsed by Los Angeles Chargers running back Austin Ekeler. This limited-edition Advanced Battlestations (ABS) PC, sponsored by Newegg, GIGABYTE, Intel, and OLOy, is available exclusively from Newegg in a bundle with a GIGABYTE monitor, with all sales benefiting the Austin Ekeler Foundation. Only 30 units of this unique gaming system, which features Ekeler's jersey number, will be sold, combining high-performance components with a custom design inspired by the NFL star.

feed.title: Ethereum Merge Not Yet Boosting Crypto Stocks
feed.summary: The Ethereum merge has not yet boosted crypto-correlated stocks, despite being a highly anticipated event. This is largely because most publicly traded crypto miners focus on Bitcoin rather than Ether. However, there's ongoing hope that the merge will have positive long-term effects on crypto equities and ETFs like SATO and BLKC, partly by benefiting companies involved in staking, such as Coinbase, and GPU manufacturers like Nvidia and AMD.

feed.title: Ethereum Merge Not Yet Boosting Crypto Stocks
feed.summary: The Ethereum merge has not yet boosted crypto-correlated stocks, including holdings in ETFs like SATO and BLKC, partly because most public crypto miners focus on Bitcoin. However, the switch to staking could benefit companies like Coinbase through increased revenue from staking services, potentially offering longer-term upsides for crypto equities and related ETFs. The article notes that ETH mining equipment is cheaper and more flexible than BTC mining equipment, using GPUs, which could benefit companies like Nvidia and AMD.

feed.title: The Chips That Make Taiwan the Center of the World
feed.summary: This article examines Taiwan's critical role in the global semiconductor industry, particularly through TSMC, and the geopolitical risks associated with its dependence on the island amid rising U.S.-China tensions. It explores the potential catastrophic economic consequences for the world if military action or political pressure disrupts Taiwan's chip manufacturing capabilities. The author argues that while Taiwan's chip industry provides a "silicon shield," it also makes the island a crucial target in any conflict.

feed.title: MICLEDI Microdisplays and Kura Technologies Announce Partnership on Custom Micro-LED Display for Ultra High-Performance AR glasses, Scaling with Partners Including GlobalFoundries and TSMC
feed.summary: MICLEDI Microdisplays and Kura Technologies have partnered to develop custom micro-LED displays for high-performance AR glasses. Kura's next-generation AR glasses will incorporate MICLEDI's 300mm microLED displays, with both companies leveraging expertise from partners like GlobalFoundries and TSMC for scaled production. This collaboration aims to bring advanced 8K AR glasses to market sooner by overcoming typical manufacturing challenges.

feed.title: IBM to invest $20 billion in New York's Hudson Valley region over 10 years
feed.summary: IBM announced plans to invest $20 billion in New York's Hudson Valley region over the next 10 years, coinciding with a visit from President Joe Biden to its Poughkeepsie site. This investment aims to boost the technology ecosystem in New York, focusing on semiconductors, computers, hybrid cloud, artificial intelligence, and quantum computing. IBM anticipates direct benefits from the CHIPS and Science Act, ensuring a stable supply of next-generation chips.

feed.title: AMD Chief Shares Her Secret Sauce Behind Successful Zen CPUs & Company Turnaround
feed.summary: Dr. Lisa Su, CEO of AMD, discussed the company's performance, industry trends, and the importance of employee productivity in an interview. She highlighted her focus on product development, analyzing market inflection points, and strong customer relationships as key to AMD's turnaround. Dr. Su also emphasized "extreme communication" and the need for product differentiation in a competitive industry, believing the high-performance and adaptive computing markets will see increased demand.

feed.title: Big data helps supercharge materials discovery
feed.summary: Toyota is leveraging big data and machine learning through its Wavebase project to accelerate materials discovery, particularly for automotive components like catalysts, batteries, and magnets. This approach aims to reduce reliance on rare metals, cut costs, and improve performance by systematically analyzing material data and predicting new material candidates. The Wavebase system, which uses sophisticated algorithms and visualization tools, is also being extended to benefit the broader Japanese industry and enable data sharing for collaborative research.

feed.title: U.S. aims to hobble China’s chip industry with sweeping new export rules
feed.summary: The Biden administration has enacted sweeping new export controls designed to significantly impede China's semiconductor industry. These rules aim to cut China off from certain chips made with U.S. tools globally and restrict the sale of advanced chip-making equipment, potentially setting China's chip manufacturing back by years. The U.S. acknowledges that the effectiveness of these unilateral controls depends on allied nations adopting similar measures.

feed.title: 10 Best Laptops For Data Science And Data Analysis
feed.summary: This article identifies the key specifications required for laptops used in data science and data analysis, such as ample RAM, fast processors, SSD storage, and CUDA-capable graphics cards. It then lists and describes 10 HP laptop models, including the HP ENVY 17t, HP Pavilion, and HP ZBook 15 G5, highlighting their suitability for various data-intensive tasks and user budgets. The guide emphasizes that while cloud solutions are common, local machine power remains crucial for data professionals.

feed.title: U.S. aims to hobble China's chip industry with sweeping new export rules
feed.summary: The Biden administration has enacted broad new export controls to impede China's chip industry, specifically targeting its access to advanced semiconductors made with U.S. equipment. These measures expand prior restrictions and are designed to slow Beijing's technological and military advancements, although their effectiveness relies on international cooperation. The rules are expected to significantly impact China's chip manufacturing capabilities, potentially setting the industry back years.

feed.title: Chip stocks sink to lowest since 2020 as U.S. expands China curbs
feed.summary: Shares of semiconductor companies experienced a significant decline globally, hitting their lowest levels since 2020, following new U.S. restrictions on China's access to American technology. This sell-off was exacerbated by a disappointing start to the earnings season, intensifying concerns about an industry downturn. The measures include curbs on chips used in AI and supercomputing, and tighter rules on semiconductor equipment sales to Chinese entities, prompting fears of retaliation from Beijing and further negative impacts on the global semiconductor sector.

feed.title: AMD CEO Lisa Su doesn't think U.S. chip export controls will hurt her company in the short term
feed.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su believes that new U.S. export controls on chips to China will not significantly harm her company's business in the short term, despite China accounting for 25% of AMD's revenue. She emphasizes a long-term belief in increased technology demand and the necessity of navigating geopolitical challenges. Su also highlights the importance of supply chain diversity, citing the CHIPS and Science Act as beneficial for U.S. manufacturing.

feed.title: Intel Plans to Lay Off Thousands of Employees As the Chipmaker Looks to Trim Costs
feed.summary: Intel Corp. is preparing for significant cost-cutting layoffs, potentially affecting thousands of employees, in response to a struggling personal-computer market and declining demand for its processors. The reductions are expected to be announced around the time of its third-quarter earnings report on October 27, with some divisions facing cuts of up to 20% of staff. This move reflects Intel's efforts to trim expenses, improve profit margins, and regain its competitive edge amidst a broader tech spending downturn.

feed.title: Cadence and Google Cloud Collaborating to Advance Chip Design Ecosystem
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems Inc. and Google Cloud are partnering to enhance system and semiconductor design by making Cadence's cloud-ready tools, part of the Cadence Cloud Passport, available and certified for Google Cloud. This collaboration promises up to a 10X improvement in design and verification throughput, with Cadence tools showing performance gains of up to 25% on Google Cloud C2D instances compared to on-premise infrastructure. Customers like Recogni have already experienced significant benefits, including scalability and accelerated project timelines for their Vision Inference Silicon development.

feed.title: Heterogeneous Integration Co-Design Won’t Be Easy
feed.summary: Heterogeneous integration, which involves combining smaller chiplets into a system-in-package (SiP), is becoming crucial for advanced silicon chip design, reducing dependence on Moore's Law. This approach requires seamless co-design and collaboration across engineering teams, utilizing advanced EDA tools for electrical, thermal, and mechanical analysis. Successful implementation hinges on optimizing chip design, process, and packaging together, with the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) standard facilitating this complex integration.

feed.title: TSMC cuts capex on tool delays, demand woes; cautious on outlook
feed.summary: TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker, has cut its 2022 capital expenditure by at least 10% and issued a cautious outlook for demand, predicting a chip downturn next year due to rising inflation and other macroeconomic factors. Despite an 80% surge in third-quarter profit, the company expects the semiconductor industry to decline in 2023, though TSMC itself anticipates a growth year. The company cited tool delivery challenges and capacity optimization as reasons for the capex reduction and stated that new U.S. export controls on advanced chip manufacturing for China would have a limited impact on its operations.

feed.title: Strive U.S. Semiconductor ETF
feed.summary: The Strive U.S. Semiconductor ETF (SHOC) invests in U.S. listed semiconductor companies, tracking the Bloomberg US Listed Semiconductors Select Index. The ETF has an expense ratio of 0.40% and has shown significant performance, with a 1-year return of 123.84%. Its top holdings include NVIDIA, Broadcom, and ASML Holding.

feed.title: Cadence, Google Cloud team on system, semiconductor design ...
feed.summary: Cadence has partnered with Google Cloud to accelerate system and semiconductor design using cloud-ready tools, resulting in up to a 10x improvement in design and verification throughput. Cadence tools, including the Cloud Passport, have been certified for Google Cloud, with Google silicon teams already utilizing them for developing Tensor SoCs and TPUs. This collaboration aims to provide engineers with enhanced scalability, access to state-of-the-art HPC infrastructure, and faster time to solution.

feed.title: The Sweeping Impact of New U.S. Semiconductor Restrictions
feed.summary: New U.S. restrictions on semiconductor sales, chipmaking equipment, and supercomputer parts are significantly impacting China's chip industry, causing a potential brain drain as American personnel face tough choices. While these policies aim to curb China's technological ambitions, they are also a "double-edged sword" for U.S. companies that rely on the Chinese market. Experts suggest these measures could accelerate China's push for self-sufficiency in semiconductors and shift focus towards mature chip technologies.

feed.title: Samsung, Qualcomm unveil industry’s fastest LPDDR5X DRAM chip
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics Co. has successfully demonstrated the performance of its LPDDR5X DRAM on Qualcomm’s Snapdragon mobile platform, achieving an industry-leading speed of 8.5 gigabits per second (Gbps). This advancement surpasses Samsung's previous record and solidifies its leadership in the memory market. The new high-speed, low-power memory is expected to enhance mobile, gaming, camera, and AI applications and find broader use in PCs, HPC, servers, and autonomous driving.

feed.title: Supermicro Extends Best of Breed Server Building Block Solutions to Include a Broad Set of OCP Technologies - Driving Customer Innovation and Time-to-Market
feed.summary: Supermicro is expanding its server portfolio to incorporate open hardware and open-source technologies, aiming to drive innovation and reduce proprietary lock-in for customers. These solutions, including an 8U 8-GPU AI training system and OCP 3.0 compliant AIOM cards, integrate best-in-class components like Intel, AMD, and ARM CPUs and NVIDIA GPUs, alongside open technologies such as OpenBMC and Open BIOS. This initiative provides customers with high-performance, efficient, and flexible server options for various applications, from cloud and AI/ML to storage and 5G/Edge computing.

feed.title: ASML reports strong Q3, unaffected by U.S. China sanctions
feed.summary: ASML Holding NV posted better-than-expected third-quarter sales and profit, along with record new bookings of 8.9 billion euros. The company, a crucial equipment supplier for chip manufacturers like TSMC, Samsung, and Intel, stated that it anticipates no significant impact from U.S. sanctions on China. Despite global macroeconomic concerns and weakness in memory chips, ASML's demand remains strong, with a backlog exceeding 30 billion euros, prompting plans to expand production capacity by 2025.

feed.title: The 5x Spike & Now Implosion of Generac’s Stock Shows What Went Horribly Wrong in the Markets since Feb. 2020
feed.summary: This article by Wolf Richter on Wolf Street examines the implosion of Generac Holdings (GNRC) stock, which surged five-fold from February 2020 to November 2021 before dropping 79% from its peak. Richter uses Generac as a case study to demonstrate the "stock-market craziness" fueled by factors like ZIRP and QE policies, and the subsequent brutal unwinding of these inflated valuations. The piece also criticizes Wall Street analysts for maintaining "buy" ratings even after significant stock drops, and discusses the broader implications for dip-buyers and the market as a whole.

feed.title: KIOXIA's next-gen EDSFF E1.S SSDs for hyperscale data centers released
feed.summary: KIOXIA has unveiled its new XD7P Series SSDs, which are designed for hyperscale and general server applications using the EDSFF E1.S form factor. These second-generation E1.S SSDs come with improved performance, including up to double the sequential write and random read/write performance compared to their predecessors. While initially available in PCIe 4.0 variants, KIOXIA has PCIe 5.0 versions under development, to be released based on customer demand.

feed.title: Why Are Semiconductor Stocks Getting Crushed?
feed.summary: Semiconductor stocks have experienced significant losses in 2022 due to growing recession fears, oversupply from pandemic-driven demand, and new strict U.S. export restrictions on sales to China. These restrictions, particularly impacting high-performance chips and manufacturing equipment, are expected to cost several firms hundreds of millions to billions in revenue. While the near-term outlook is dim, the long-term growth expectation for the semiconductor market remains intact, despite anticipated cyclical downturns.

feed.title: US weighs China tech restrictions on quantum computing and AI
feed.summary: The Biden administration is considering new export controls to limit China's access to emerging computing technologies like quantum computing and artificial intelligence. These potential restrictions follow earlier measures aimed at curbing China's use of advanced semiconductors for military and surveillance purposes. The move risks further escalating tensions with China and forcing other nations to choose between the two economic powers.

feed.title: China’s Biren GPUs Can’t Be Made at TSMC Due To US Regulations & Competition With NVIDIA
feed.summary: Birentech, a Chinese chip startup, is reportedly barred from manufacturing its Biren GPUs at TSMC due to US regulations aimed at restricting China's access to advanced AI and datacenter chips. This decision is influenced by the Biren BR100's potential to outperform NVIDIA's A100 chips, which are already banned for the Chinese market, and could severely impact Birentech's manufacturing plans.

feed.title: Walmart sets the stage for crypto payments
feed.summary: Walmart is moving towards adopting cryptocurrencies as a payment option for its customers and plans to create NFTs in the metaverse, according to its Global Chief of Technology. This move could significantly bridge the gap between cryptocurrencies and mainstream consumers. The article also touches on other crypto news, including institutional adoption by Fidelity, legal issues with NFT project Slotie, Solana's continued blockchain outages, new crypto regulations in the UK, and Kraken's compliance with EU sanctions against Russia.

feed.title: Samsung Elec to expand foundry business to tackle TSMC
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics plans to significantly expand its foundry business, particularly focusing on mature and specialty nodes, to challenge TSMC's dominance. The South Korean tech giant aims to more than double its production capacity for these chip types by 2027, targeting areas like automotive and consumer electronics where TSMC currently holds over 50% of the market share. This strategic shift comes as Samsung looks to increase its overall market share in the foundry sector, where it has historically lagged behind TSMC despite advancements in leading-edge technologies.

feed.title: MKS Instruments Maintains Quarterly Dividend at $0.22 Per Share; Payable Dec. 9 to Shareholders of Record on Nov. 28
feed.summary: MKS Instruments has announced that it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.22 per share. The dividend is set to be payable on December 9 to shareholders of record as of November 28. This decision reflects the company's continued commitment to returning value to its shareholders.

feed.title: Why Are Semiconductor Shares Being Crushed?
feed.summary: Semiconductor stocks are facing significant declines in 2022 due to multiple factors, including recession fears, weak PC sales, and strict US export restrictions on sales to China. These restrictions are expected to heavily impact major companies like Nvidia and AMD, although the long-term growth outlook for the overall semiconductor market remains positive, albeit with near-term uncertainties.

feed.title: How to design wireless charging systems for safe, reliable operation
feed.summary: This article details how to design wireless charging systems for safe and reliable operation, addressing challenges like space limitations, robust protection from ESD and overloads, and power efficiency. It provides recommendations for circuit protection, efficient control, and safety sensing for power adapters, charging cables, and charging pads, ensuring compliance with international safety standards like Qi and USB protocols. The article also emphasizes the importance of early engagement with manufacturers' application engineers to streamline the design process.

feed.title: Mobileye pops more than 37% in market debut after spinning out of Intel
feed.summary: Mobileye, Intel's self-driving car technology company, saw its shares surge over 37% in its stock market debut after spinning out of Intel. The IPO, which valued Mobileye at around $17 billion, raised $861 million, with Intel planning to use some proceeds to build chip factories. This market debut provides a notable tech IPO in a slow year, despite Mobileye's valuation being below Intel's initial expectations.

feed.title: AMD Issues Performance Advisory for Ryzen 7000 Processors
feed.summary: AMD has released a performance advisory for its new Ryzen 7000 processors, acknowledging reports of unexpected performance variations in certain games. The company is actively investigating these issues and plans to collaborate with game developers and hardware partners for optimization. AMD also addressed concerns about Windows 11 performance, stating that their testing showed no significant differences compared to Windows 10.

feed.title: Arm Could Change Licensing Model to Charge OEMs Directly
feed.summary: Arm might change its licensing model by requiring OEMs to license almost all of its IP if they wish to use its CPU IP, directly challenging existing deals and potentially pushing chipmakers toward alternatives like RISC-V. This potential shift, revealed through Qualcomm's counterclaim in a legal dispute, would prevent mixing Arm's IP with other companies' designs (e.g., AMD GPUs in Samsung chips). The change is expected to take effect in 2025, with exemptions for long-term licensees like NVIDIA and Apple.

feed.title: Semiconductors: Three-way Contest In 2022, Plus Predictions For 2023 And Beyond
feed.summary: The semiconductor industry saw a three-way contest in 2022, with TSMC topping the third quarter, while Intel is expected to finish a distant third for the full year. Although a chip downturn is predicted for 2023, growth is expected to return in 2024, with the industry potentially reaching $1 trillion in sales by 2032. The author emphasizes the importance of India approving silicon fab proposals to capitalize on the upcoming upcycle in the middle of the decade.

feed.title: At NetApp INSIGHT 2022, Dive Into NVIDIA DGX BasePOD and NetApp ONTAP AI
feed.summary: This article announces NVIDIA's participation as a sponsor at NetApp INSIGHT 2022, highlighting solutions for operationalizing AI use cases. It introduces NVIDIA DGX BasePOD as a foundation for AI infrastructure, including NetApp ONTAP AI, and discusses how these solutions help businesses deploy proven models faster. The piece also outlines NVIDIA DGX-Ready Managed Services for managing AI infrastructure and filling IT skills gaps.

feed.title: Vishay buys SiC MOSFET firm for $50m
feed.summary: Vishay Intertechnology has acquired MaxPower Semiconductor, a silicon carbide MOSFET supplier, for $50 million. MaxPower's IP portfolio, including over 100 patents and proprietary device structures for SiC MOSFETs, will be integrated into Vishay’s MOSFET segment to support high-voltage electrification applications. This strategic acquisition aims to expand Vishay's product portfolio and strengthen its competitive position in fast-growing markets like electric vehicles.

feed.title: Micron Ships World's Most Advanced DRAM Technology With 1-Beta Node
feed.summary: Micron Technology has announced it is shipping qualification samples of its 1β (1-beta) DRAM technology to select smartphone manufacturers and chipset partners, achieving mass production readiness for the world's most advanced DRAM technology node. This LPDDR5X mobile memory delivers top speeds of 8.5 gigabits per second, with significant gains in power efficiency (15% improvement) and bit density (over 35% improvement) at 16Gb per die capacity. The 1β node is poised to support highly responsive applications from mobile to data centers and intelligent vehicles, stemming from Micron's pioneering R&D and nanomanufacturing capabilities.

feed.title: High-density MRAM family now commercially available
feed.summary: Everspin Technologies Inc. has commercially launched its EMxxLX family of high-density MRAM devices, touted as the highest performing persistent memory available. These MRAM devices offer superior performance compared to traditional memories like SRAM and NOR flash, suitable for diverse applications including industrial IoT, aerospace, and medical systems. The technology boasts low latency, non-volatility, high endurance, and radiation resistance, with densities ranging from 8 to 64 megabits.

feed.title: AMD Unveils World’s Most Advanced Gaming Graphics Cards, Built on Groundbreaking AMD RDNA 3 Architecture with Chiplet Design
feed.summary: AMD has introduced its new Radeon RX 7900 XTX and Radeon RX 7900 XT graphics cards, based on the RDNA 3 architecture featuring a chiplet design. These cards are poised to deliver exceptional 4K and higher resolution gaming performance with significant improvements in performance per watt, AI throughput, and raytracing compared to the previous generation. AMD also announced updates to its software suite, including FSR 2.2 and the upcoming FSR 3, alongside the expansion of the AMD Advantage framework to desktop PCs.

feed.title: BDA advises onsemi on sale of Niigata foundry to Mercuria, Sangyo Sosei, Fukuoka Capital
feed.summary: BDA Partners advised onsemi on the sale of its Niigata wafer manufacturing facility in Japan to investment vehicles managed by Mercuria Holdings Co Ltd, Sangyo Sosei Advisory Inc, and Fukuoka Capital Partners Co Ltd. This transaction will create Japan's first independent specialty semiconductor foundry. The sale is expected to close upon regulatory approvals and fulfillment of closing conditions.

feed.title: How Taiwan won the semiconductor race
feed.summary: This article explains how Taiwan became a dominant force in the global semiconductor industry, despite starting as an agricultural economy. It attributes Taiwan's success to globalization's impact on electronics, East Asia's skilled workforce and infrastructure, and a unique business model focused on contract manufacturing rather than brand development. The piece highlights TSMC's role in supplying advanced chips globally, creating a significant dependency on Taiwan.

feed.title: OSS Delivers Ruggedized Compute Solution for Embark's Commercial Autonomous Truck Deployment
feed.summary: One Stop Systems, Inc. (OSS) has secured two program wins with Embark Trucks, Inc., an autonomous trucking technology developer, to provide ruggedized compute solutions for its commercial autonomous truck deployment. These wins include the Centauri rugged PCIe storage expansion system and the 3U SDS-AT rugged accelerated computing server, both designed to withstand harsh environments and support critical autonomous driving operations. This partnership will equip Embark’s test fleet with high-performance computing and transportable storage capabilities, contributing to the advancement of autonomous long-haul trucking.

feed.title: Intel Says Its On Track With “Aggressive” 2030 Foundry Goals, Aiming The 2nd Spot By The End of The Decade
feed.summary: Intel is aggressively pursuing its 2030 foundry goals, aiming to become the world's second-largest chip manufacturer, surpassing Samsung and closing in on TSMC. The company is investing significantly in new fabrication plants globally, developing next-gen process technologies, and building capacity to attract clients and diversify the semiconductor supply chain. Intel plans to achieve this through strategic investments, global expansion, and strong competition against its rivals.

feed.title: Samsung, LG gear up for launch of 8K gaming monitors
feed.summary: Samsung and LG are preparing to launch 8K gaming monitors, with Samsung unveiling its Odyssey Neo G9 at CES 2023. This move comes as gamers seek larger, clearer screens and high-performance GPUs enable 8K quality. However, high prices and a lack of compatible games remain challenges for mainstream adoption, despite an expected significant growth in the 8K device market.

feed.title: What to expect during AMD’s 4th Gen EPYC Genoa Release: Join theCUBE Nov. 10
feed.summary: AMD is set to release its 4th Gen EPYC Genoa CPU on November 10, entering a competitive x86 processor market. The new chips are expected to feature 96 cores and 192 threads based on a 5-nanometer manufacturing process, offering higher density and power efficiency. This launch positions AMD ahead of competitor Intel, which has delayed its Xeon Scalable CPUs.

feed.title: Samsung to only use Qualcomm’s AP for Galaxy S23 series
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics will exclusively use Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 mobile application processors for its entire Galaxy S23 series, scheduled for release in mid-February. This decision aims to address quality controversies, as previous Galaxy S models used different APs based on region. The move highlights Samsung's strategy to strengthen its premium market presence amidst a decline in overall smartphone shipments but growth in high-end device sales.

feed.title: Exclusive: Nvidia offers new advanced chip for China that meets U.S. export controls
feed.summary: Nvidia has developed a new advanced chip, the A800, specifically for the Chinese market, which complies with recent U.S. export control rules designed to restrict cutting-edge technology to China. This move by Nvidia (NVDA.O) is a strategic effort to maintain its presence in the significant Chinese market after U.S. regulations impacted sales of its A100 chip, with the A800 featuring a reduced data transfer rate to meet the export requirements. Analysts suggest this is a "repackaged A100 GPU" demonstrating Nvidia's adaptability to trade restrictions.

feed.title: HPE Expands Supercomputing Portfolio with New HPE Cray EX and XD Systems
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has expanded its supercomputing portfolio with new HPE Cray EX and HPE Cray XD systems, designed to make high-performance computing more accessible to enterprises. These new systems leverage HPE's exascale innovation to offer enhanced performance and AI-at-scale capabilities in smaller, more energy-efficient form factors and at lower price points. This expansion aims to help various industries, including energy, financial services, health, life sciences, and manufacturing, accelerate R&D, improve time-to-market, and strengthen competitiveness through advanced modeling, simulation, and AI.

feed.title: Nvidia’s new chip is tailored for Chinese market and meets US export regulations
feed.summary: Nvidia has introduced a new A800 GPU specifically designed for the Chinese market to comply with US export regulations, which previously banned sales of its A100 and H100 chips without a license. This move allows Nvidia to continue serving its Chinese customers, who represent about a quarter of its gross sales, while adhering to US government’s restrictions on advanced chip technology. The A800 is an alternative product engineered to meet reduced export control requirements and cannot be programmed to exceed them.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries delivers record earnings on sustained demand for lower-end commodity chips
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries Inc. reported record third-quarter financial results, exceeding Wall Street expectations due to sustained demand for lower-end commodity chips, particularly in the automotive and IoT industries. The company achieved record revenue of $2.07 billion and an impressive operating margin of 17.2%, despite concerns in the broader chip market. GlobalFoundries also provided optimistic guidance for the fourth quarter, anticipating strong earnings and revenue.

feed.title: Intel debuts powerful Max Series CPUs and GPUs for high-performance computing and AI
feed.summary: Intel has launched its new Max Series CPUs and GPUs, designed to compete in the high-performance computing (HPC) and artificial intelligence (AI) markets against rivals like AMD and Nvidia. These new chips will power the upcoming Aurora supercomputer at Argonne National Laboratory, aiming to make HPC and AI more accessible for solving complex global challenges. Intel claims its Max Series offers significant performance advantages and better power efficiency compared to competitive silicon.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Extends Supercomputing to the Enterprise with New HPE Cray Portfolio
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is expanding its supercomputing portfolio with new HPE Cray EX and HPE Cray XD supercomputers, making exascale-class technology more accessible and affordable for enterprises. These systems are designed to help businesses across various industries, including energy, finance, healthcare, and manufacturing, accelerate R&D, AI, and machine learning initiatives. The new offerings provide significant performance and AI-at-scale capabilities in smaller form factors, supporting advanced modeling, simulation, and data-intensive workloads in traditional data centers.

feed.title: AMD launches data center CPU 'Genoa', taps Google, Microsoft as customers (Nov. 10)
feed.summary: AMD has launched its fourth-generation EPYC processor, codenamed "Genoa," for data centers, securing major customers like Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, and Oracle. This new chip offers significant performance and energy efficiency improvements over its predecessor, translating into lower operational costs for enterprises and cloud data centers. The launch comes as rival Intel faces delays with its own data center chip, and AMD continues to gain market share, boosting its stock by about 14%.

feed.title: Micron Ships Data Center-Class DDR5, Ready For New AMD EPYC CPUs
feed.summary: Micron Technology announced it is shipping its new data center-class DDR5 SDRAM memory in volume, coinciding with the release of AMD's new Zen 4 EPYC processors. This transition from DDR4 to DDR5 is described as a "once-in-a-decade change," crucial for handling the increasing data volumes and performance demands of modern data centers. The new DDR5 memory offers increased bandwidth, more channels, and improved power efficiency compared to its predecessor.

feed.title: Enovix names Ajay Marathe COO By Investing.com
feed.summary: Enovix Corp (NASDAQ:ENVX), a 3D Silicon Lithium-Ion battery company, has appointed Ajay Marathe as its new Chief Operating Officer (COO), reporting to CEO Harold Rust. Marathe brings 38 years of experience from major technology companies like Western Digital and Advanced Micro Devices. The announcement led to a 7.2% increase in ENVX shares during Thursday's trading.

feed.title: HPE's low-end supercomputers take aim at the AI market
feed.summary: HPE and Dell are introducing new supercomputing and high-performance computing (HPC) systems designed to meet the growing demands of AI-driven workloads. HPE is focusing on smaller, lower-cost supercomputers with advanced cooling, while Dell is enhancing its HPC offerings with powerful GPUs, aiming to make supercomputing capabilities more accessible to a broader range of enterprise users. This convergence of supercomputing and HPC is blurring the lines between the two, as both companies compete to provide infrastructure that can efficiently handle complex AI, modeling, and simulation tasks.

feed.title: AMD Announces Completion of Class B Qualification for First Space-Grade Versal Adaptive SoCs Enabling On-Board AI Processing in Space
feed.summary: AMD has announced the completion of Class B qualification for its first space-grade Versal™ adaptive SoCs, the XQR Versal AI Core XQRVC1902 devices. These radiation-tolerant chips are designed for on-board AI processing and high-bandwidth signal processing in satellites and space applications, with shipments expected to begin in early 2023. This development offers a more flexible and cost-effective alternative to custom ASICs for complex AI applications in space, allowing for in-flight reprogramming and adaptation to harsh radiation environments.

feed.title: AMD announces its reprogrammable Versal AI chips are ready for space-based deployments
feed.summary: AMD's reprogrammable Versal AI Core chip has achieved Class B qualification, making it suitable for space-based deployments in commercial satellites. This radiation-resistant SoC enables significant advancements in onboard AI processing for satellites, offering greater performance and reconfigurability compared to traditional ASICs. Raytheon Co. is an early adopter, planning to use these chips for its next-generation space processors.

feed.title: Jeff McBride's "Gauss Speedway" Propels Magnetic Cars Along a Clever "Stepper Motor" PCB
feed.summary: Electronics engineer Jeff McBride has created the "Gauss Speedway," a compact racetrack that uses an STM32F051 microcontroller and Rust firmware to propel 3D-printed magnetic cars. The system functions like a flat stepper motor, utilizing magnetic fields to move cars along the track, complete with guard rails for centering. McBride notes its clever design, though acknowledges its inefficiency, drawing parallels to Kevin Lynagh's micro-robotics project but optimized for high-speed racing.

feed.title: AMD Powers Aisin Next-Generation Automated Parking Assist System
feed.summary: AMD announced that its Xilinx Automotive (XA) Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC platform will power Aisin's next-generation Automated Parking-Assist (APA) system, set to ship in model year 2024 vehicles. The platform's deep learning processor and high-performance capabilities enable low-latency AI-based image processing for pedestrian and vehicle detection, allowing the APA system to autonomously park vehicles and perform emergency braking. This collaboration highlights AMD's growing role in the automotive industry, providing adaptable solutions for advanced driver-assistance and autonomous driving applications.

feed.title: On-Chip Power Distribution Modeling Becomes Essential Below 7nm
feed.summary: Modeling power distribution is increasingly critical for SoC and 3D-IC designs, particularly at advanced nodes below 7nm, due to rising power density, lower operating voltages, and thinner metal layers. Accurate analysis is essential to prevent timing and functional failures, especially with the complexities introduced by electromigration, simultaneous switching, and the sheer scale of current designs. This necessitates more sophisticated EDA tools and earlier integration of power modeling in the design flow to address potential issues before tape-out.

feed.title: Industry Ministry, U.S. Firm to Cooperate on Semiconductor Industry Development
feed.summary: Indonesia's Industry Ministry is partnering with a United States company, Zerro Power, co-founded by innovator Sehat Sutardja, to establish an integrated circuit design center. This collaboration aims to reduce Indonesia's dependence on semiconductor imports and develop an independent national semiconductor industry. The initiative is part of broader efforts to secure the semiconductor supply chain amidst increasing global demand.

feed.title: GeForce RTX 4080 Out Now: Beyond Fast For Gamers & Creators
feed.summary: NVIDIA has released the GeForce RTX 4080 graphics card, featuring its 3rd generation RTX architecture, making it available through NVIDIA and various add-in card providers and system integrators. The RTX 4080 offers significant performance improvements over its predecessor, the RTX 3080 Ti, especially in ray-traced games, while maintaining efficient power consumption. It includes advanced features like NVIDIA DLSS 3, dual 8th gen NVIDIA Encoders for video, and AV1 support, enhancing experiences for both gamers and creators.

feed.title: AMD chip to power auto supplier Aisin’s new automated car parking system
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) announced that its Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC microprocessor will power a new automated parking system developed by Japan's Aisin Corp. This system aims to make automated parking more mainstream by offering a cost-effective solution for automakers. The use of AMD's FPGA-based chip, acquired from Xilinx, will allow for post-sale software and chip updates in vehicles, with production expected to begin on 2024 models.

feed.title: AMD and Analog Devices Resolve Patent Infringement Lawsuits
feed.summary: AMD and Analog Devices, Inc. have announced the resolution of all their ongoing patent litigations, based on mutually agreed-upon terms. As a result, the two companies plan to collaborate on technology to develop next-generation solutions for their communications and data center customers.

feed.title: Intel FakeCatcher detects deepfakes with a 96% accuracy rate
feed.summary: Intel has launched FakeCatcher, a deepfake detection platform that boasts a 96% accuracy rate and provides real-time results. Unlike other solutions, FakeCatcher identifies genuine human characteristics like subtle blood flow signals in video pixels to distinguish real content from AI-generated fakes. This technology aims to restore trust in media and can be used by news organizations, social media platforms, and non-profit groups to combat the spread of manipulated videos.

feed.title: Competition gets heated on Fungible’s DPU patch
feed.summary: Fungible, a data processing unit (DPU) startup founded in 2015, is facing intense competition from major players like AMD, Intel, Kalray, Marvell, and Nvidia. Despite developing impressive DPU chips and acquiring composability software, Fungible has struggled to secure major customers or OEM deals and recently pivoted away from composable systems to focus on its storage technology. The company reportedly tried to raise more funding unsuccessfully and is now in a precarious position amidst rumors of acquisition.

feed.title: Exxact Corp. Works with NVIDIA to Support Omniverse Enterprise
feed.summary: Exxact Corporation, a high-performance computing provider, is now offering integrated solutions to the media and entertainment industry to support NVIDIA Omniverse Enterprise. This collaboration provides studios with workstations powered by NVIDIA RTX GPUs and Omniverse Enterprise, enabling real-time collaborative 3D scene creation and simulation. Exxact aims to enhance productivity, communication, and creativity for M&E clients, particularly as remote production models become more prevalent.

feed.title: Intel Foundry head steps down, as contract chip making division tries to ramp up
feed.summary: Randhir Thakur, the head of Intel's Foundry business, has resigned to pursue other opportunities, a move that comes amidst company-wide layoffs and spending cuts. He will remain until early 2023 to assist with the leadership transition and the acquisition of Tower Semiconductor. This departure impacts Intel's efforts to grow its contract chip-making division, which faces stiff competition from established leaders like TSMC and Samsung, despite securing major customer wins.

feed.title: Samsung Secures 3-nm Advanced Chip Orders From NVIDIA, Qualcomm – Report
feed.summary: Samsung Foundry has reportedly secured orders from major firms like NVIDIA, Qualcomm, IBM, and Baidu to manufacture advanced 3-nanometer chips. This move, leveraging Samsung's gate-all-around (GAA) transistor technology developed with IBM, aims to position Samsung ahead of TSMC in advanced chip manufacturing and diversify the semiconductor supply chain amidst geopolitical tensions. Deliveries in large quantities are expected to begin in early 2024.

feed.title: ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) News, Articles, Events & Latest Updates
feed.summary: This article provides news and updates related to the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW), featuring various headlines about Cathie Wood's ARK Invest activities. It details ARK's recent trades, including selling chip giants like TSMC and AMD, buying into crypto exchange Bullish, and increasing stakes in companies like Shopify, Palantir, and Cerebras. The news also covers specific stock performances and analyst sentiments related to these investments.

feed.title: Microcontrollers learn to embrace neural networks
feed.summary: Deep learning is increasingly moving to constrained edge systems like microcontrollers due to concerns about latency, bandwidth, and privacy with cloud-based processing. Chipmakers such as NXP and Silicon Labs are integrating machine learning accelerators into their microcontrollers to improve efficiency, while Microchip Technology explores future acceleration options. The article also discusses the potential future role of Transformer architectures in microcontroller-based AI, despite their current complexity.

feed.title: Silicon Labs CEO Matt Johnson Elected Chair of SIA
feed.summary: Silicon Labs President and CEO Matt Johnson has been elected Chair of the Semiconductor Industry Association (SIA) Board of Directors, with Texas Instruments Chairman, President, and CEO Rich Templeton elected as Vice Chair. They will lead the SIA in 2023, focusing on implementing the CHIPS and Science Act and promoting government policies that support the semiconductor ecosystem. Both leaders bring significant industry experience and will advocate for the industry's interests in Washington and globally.

feed.title: A Rising Star in the Biden Administration Faces a $100 Billion Test
feed.summary: Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo is spearheading a massive industrial policy initiative, distributing nearly $100 billion to bolster the U.S. semiconductor industry and expand broadband access. This effort aims to enhance American competitiveness in future technologies like AI and secure chip supplies, while also navigating a growing economic rivalry with China. Her success in this endeavor will significantly impact the future of the U.S. economy.

feed.title: Supermicro W790 E-ATX Motherboard Leaks Out: Sapphire Rapids-WS CPU Support, 16 ‘8-Channel’ DDR5 DIMMs, Lots of PCIe Gen5 Slots
feed.summary: A Supermicro W790 E-ATX motherboard supporting Intel's Sapphire Rapids-WS Xeon Workstation CPUs has leaked, revealing key features. The X13SWA-TF motherboard includes an LGA 4677 socket, 16 DDR5 DIMM slots with 8-channel memory support, and six PCIe Gen5 x16 slots. This new platform, known as Fishhawk Falls, is anticipated for workstation and HEDT PC builds, with CPU launches expected by early 2023.

feed.title: AWS re:Invent: AWS Enters HPC Market, Competing with Partners Intel, Nvidia, and AMD
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is expanding into the high-performance computing (HPC) market with new Graviton chips designed in-house. This move pits AWS against its chipmaking partners like Intel, Nvidia, and AMD, aiming to offer more cost-effective computing power for tasks such as weather forecasting and gene sequencing. AWS emphasized that while Graviton will compete, they will continue to offer services based on other companies' chips, maintaining their partnerships.

feed.title: STMicro Unveils 6-axis IMU with Embedded Sensor Fusion and AI
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has released a new 6-axis IMU, the LSM6DSV16X, designed for low-power sensing applications like AR/VR and wearables. This IMU incorporates embedded sensor fusion blocks and Machine Learning cores to efficiently handle and aggregate data from multiple sensors. The aim is to offload compute-intensive tasks from application processors, enabling more practical and lower-power sensing solutions.

feed.title: President Joe Biden to visit TSMC plant being built in north Phoenix
feed.summary: President Joe Biden is scheduled to visit Phoenix on December 6 to commemorate a key milestone, the "first tool-in," at the Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) plant in north Phoenix. This marks his first presidential visit to Arizona and highlights the administration's focus on boosting domestic semiconductor manufacturing through initiatives like the CHIPS and Science Act. The TSMC facility represents a significant $12 billion investment in Arizona, aiming to reduce the country's reliance on foreign microchip production.

feed.title: TSMC to make higher-end chips in US: sources
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) plans to produce advanced 4-nanometer chips at its new Arizona plant when it opens in 2024, an upgrade from initial plans, following pressure from US customers like Apple. The company is also expected to commit to a second plant for even more advanced 3-nanometer chips. This move aligns with US efforts to bolster domestic semiconductor manufacturing due to supply chain disruptions and geopolitical concerns, though Taiwan intends to keep its latest technology at home.

feed.title: Micron Technology to invest RM4.4bil on 2nd factory in Penang
feed.summary: Micron Technology announced a new investment of US$1 billion (RM4.4 billion) to build a second factory in Penang, adding to its existing US$1 billion investment since 2019. This expansion will bring their total factory space to 1.5 million sq ft and is expected to begin production next year. CEO Sanjay Mehrotra highlights the company's strong position in the semiconductor market despite current industry challenges, emphasizing the critical role of its Penang operations in its global network.

feed.title: Intel says it will squeeze 1 trillion transistors onto a chip package by 2030
feed.summary: Intel's researchers have revealed innovations in chip packaging that could lead to chips with over a trillion transistors by 2030, significantly extending Moore's Law. This development would result in computer chips ten times more powerful than current advanced silicon. The company aims to address the increasing demand for computing power driven by data consumption and AI.

feed.title: Lattice Extends Low Power Leadership with New Lattice Avant FPGA Platform
feed.summary: Lattice Semiconductor has launched Lattice Avant, a new FPGA platform designed to extend its low-power leadership into mid-range FPGAs. This platform offers enhanced power efficiency, advanced connectivity, and optimized computing capabilities, targeting applications in Communications, Computing, Industrial, and Automotive markets. The Avant platform, beginning with the Avant-E family, aims to double Lattice's addressable market and accelerate customer designs with superior power efficiency and performance.

feed.title: TSMC Announces Updates for TSMC Arizona
feed.summary: TSMC announced significant updates for its Arizona fabs, including starting construction on a second fab for 3nm production by 2026, bringing the total investment to approximately US$40 billion. The two fabs are projected to create 10,000 high-paying jobs and produce over 600,000 wafers annually. TSMC also plans an on-site Industrial Water Reclamation Plant for near zero liquid discharge, aiming for the greenest semiconductor manufacturing facility in the US.

feed.title: Lattice Launches Avant FPGA Platform, Avant-E Parts for Mid-Range FPGA Edge Processing Tasks
feed.summary: Lattice Semiconductor has introduced its new Avant FPGA platform, featuring the Avant-E chips designed for mid-range edge processing applications. Lattice claims significant improvements in power efficiency, performance, and connectivity over competitors, including 2.5x better power consumption and double the performance. The Avant-E FPGAs offer up to 477k logic cells and extensive DSPs, targeting on-device machine learning and AI workloads, with full support from Lattice's existing software tools.

feed.title: TSMC triples Arizona chip plant investment, Biden hails project
feed.summary: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC announced it would more than triple its investment in its new Arizona plant to $40 billion, marking one of the largest foreign investments in U.S. history. President Joe Biden praised the project as a move to bring American manufacturing back and strengthen the U.S. supply chain for semiconductors. The expanded investment includes building a second facility for advanced 3-nanometer chips by 2026, aiming to create 13,000 high-tech jobs and address semiconductor shortages.

feed.title: Apple to be TSMC US fab top customer
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is set to become the largest customer of TSMC's new factories in Arizona, as announced by Apple CEO Tim Cook. TSMC will invest $40 billion in the US fabs, including a second plant for more advanced 3-nanometer chips, with production slated to start in 2024 for 4-nanometer chips and 2026 for 3-nanometer chips. Other major chip companies like AMD and Qualcomm also plan to source chips from these new US facilities, highlighting the growing importance of domestic semiconductor manufacturing.

feed.title: ST SiC power modules for Hyundai EV platform ...
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics' SiC-MOSFET based power modules have been chosen by Hyundai Motor Company for its E-GMP electric vehicle platform, specifically powering the Kia EV6. These modules offer flexible power ratings, support common EV traction operating voltages, and feature robust sintering technology for integration. ST's third-generation SiC technology aims to provide high power density, energy efficiency, and improved vehicle performance, range, and charge time.

feed.title: The 10 Hottest Semiconductor Startup Companies Of 2022
feed.summary: Despite a turbulent year for the semiconductor industry, driven by macroeconomic hurdles, the market for AI, ML, high-power computing, cloud, and intelligent edge business is thriving. This article highlights 10 semiconductor startups making significant strides in 2022. These companies are attracting substantial investments and developing innovative solutions for next-generation technologies.

feed.title: TSMC Revenue Rises 50% in November Despite Covid Challenges
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) reported a 50% increase in revenue for November, reaching NT$222.7 billion ($7.3 billion). This growth occurred despite a challenging environment marked by declining consumer electronics demand and COVID-19 disruptions in China. TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker and exclusive supplier of Apple's Silicon chips, continues a strong sales streak amplified by pandemic-driven demand.

feed.title: AMD Radeon RX 7900 XT Review - Circuit Board Analysis
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed circuit board analysis of the AMD Radeon RX 7900 XT, focusing on its power delivery systems for the GPU and memory. It identifies specific components like the Monolithic Power Systems controllers and DrMOS, and details the Hynix GDDR6 memory chips. The review also highlights AMD's Navi 31 graphics processor, which utilizes a chiplet architecture with a Graphics Compute Die (GCD) and Memory Cache Dies (MCDs).

feed.title: New business to bring more jobs to Plainview
feed.summary: A new joint venture called DSM Semichem LLC. is set to begin construction on a facility in Plainview, Texas, in January, which will produce electronic sulfuric acid (ELSA) and create new jobs. This development is a result of Samsung's investment in a Texas facility and its need for local resources. The Plainview location will be the first U.S. facility for Dongjin Semichem Co., Ltd., and its operations are anticipated to begin after January 1, with full operation planned by the end of 2024.

feed.title: GraniteShares 2x Long NVDA Daily ETF
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed profile of the GraniteShares 2x Long NVDA Daily ETF (NVDL), offering comprehensive data on its price, dividends, expenses, holdings, and performance. It compares NVDL to alternative ETFs within its category and segment, highlighting key metrics like expense ratio, assets under management, and returns. The profile also includes technical analysis, volatility data, and recent news relevant to the ETF.

feed.title: New Intel oneAPI 2023 Tools Maximize Value of Upcoming Intel Hardware
feed.summary: Intel has released its oneAPI 2023 tools, designed to maximize the value of upcoming Intel hardware such as 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors, Intel Xeon CPU Max Series, and Intel Data Center GPUs. These tools offer enhanced performance and productivity, cross-platform compatibility through SYCL code for non-Intel GPUs, and improved support for AI and machine learning frameworks. The update aims to address the growing need for efficient multi-architecture programming in a developer landscape increasingly targeting heterogeneous systems.

feed.title: Red-Hot Solar Stock Earns 'Top Pick' Status
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has named SolarEdge Technologies Inc (NASDAQ:SEDG) as a top pick for 2023 in the solar energy industry, alongside First Solar (FSLR). This endorsement comes despite the stock being down 1.1% recently, as SEDG has shown a 37% gain this quarter and boasts strong support from its 20-day moving average. Options traders have also been overwhelmingly bullish on SEDG in the past two months, as indicated by a high call/put volume ratio.
